title: Ssi-ruuvi ion paddle beamer

The '''ion paddle beamer''' was a disk-shaped weapon carried by [[Ssi-ruu]]k [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] which was used to [[Stun/Legends|stun]] targets. [[Ion beamer]]s were originally used for medical purposes, but the [[Ssi-ruu]]k later found its usefulness as a weapon. Beings hit by such weapons were disabled and rendered unconscious for the purpose of live capture. The [[Ssi-ruu]]k race that attacked the isolated [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Bakura/Legends|Bakura]] just [[Standard day/Legends|days]] after the end of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] had a bizarre purpose; to capture living specimens to power their battle [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] through the process of [[entechment]]. Using the ion paddle beamer allowed the Ssi-ruuk to capture their targets alive.

==Technical information==
[[File:Ssi-Ruuvi_paddle_beamer_egwt.png|thumb|left|150px|Paddle beamer [[:Category:Technical drawing images|schematics]].]]
The ion paddle beamer was a short-range energy weapon that was designed to incapacitate and [[Stun/Legends|stun]] a target. The weapon was shaped like a large disk with a cylindrical [[Barrel/Legends|barrel]] terminating in a [[Beam Emitter]]. [[Ion Focusing Coil]]s wrapped around half of the barrel, with a raised section studded with [[Discharge Nozzle]]s. Designed to be used by the clawed hands of the Ssi-ruuk, the weapon featured several reinforced claw guards that allowed the wielder to hold and fire it with one hand. The weapon was fired by a simple control stick and trigger located on the underside, with the weapon's output managed by sliding power controls.<ref name="EGWT" /> 

The main rounded body of the weapon contained the [[Ion Beam Generator]] and [[Acceleration Chamber]], along with the [[Battery coil|Enteched Energy Cell]] that powered the ion paddle beamer. [[Intake|Cooling vents]] were located on either side of the weapon, running around the edges. As the Ssi-ruuk wore no [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]], ion paddle beamers were stored in shoulder bags, or attached to tool belts and [[Bandolier/Legends|bandoliers]] by a [[Magnetic Clip]].<ref name="EGWT" />

When activated, the weapon fired a thin silver [[ion beam]]. The primary purpose of the beam was to paralyze a target by affecting their [[nervous system]].<ref name="EGWT" /> This was achieved by disrupting the electrochemical signals that the rest of the body sent to the [[Brain/Legends|brain]] which in turn shut down an individual's control over their body. The ion beam needed to strike an area of high neural activity, such as the target's spine, to fully disable them.<ref name="TABSB" /> This would render the target unconscious. A grazing shot could leave a [[Limb/Legends|limb]] numb for several [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]],<ref name="EGWT" /> or even have no effect if it struck a non-vital area.<ref name="TABSB" /> The ion paddle beamer had an optimum range of eight [[Meter/Legends|meters]], and a maximum range of twelve meters. At extremely close ranges, the ion paddle beamer could kill a target.<ref name="EGWT" />

Ion paddle beamers were powered by an Enteched Energy Cell, running off of the life energy of previous Ssi-ruuk captives that had been extracted through a process called "[[entechment]]." As all Ssi-ruuk technology was based on this energy source, the weapon allowed the [[Alien/Legends|aliens]] to capture live [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] who were then put through the entechment process.<ref name="EGWT" /> Like all Ssi-ruuvi power cells, the entechment energy fluctuated, but the ion paddle beamer was designed to compensate for the unpredictable power feed using an unusual circuitry configuration.<ref name="TABSB" />

A particularly dangerous aspect of these weapons was the ability for the ion beam to bypass conventional [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] and [[Armor/Legends|armor]]. Even [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] were unable to deflect the ion beams; instead, the beam bent around the blade. [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] eventually found a way to deflect an ion beam with a lightsaber by adjusting the blade's frequency, but it came at the cost of rendering the weapon incapable of deflecting conventional [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] bolts.<ref name="EGWT" />

==History==
[[File:Paddbeam.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An ion paddle beamer in the hands of a Ssi-ruuvi]]
Ion paddle beamers were originally medical tools before the Ssi-ruuk repurposed them into effective short range weapons.<ref name="NEGWT">''[[The New Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]''</ref> The Ssi-ruuk and their [[P'w'eck]] [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] used these weapons when they attempted to [[Battle of Bakura|capture]] the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bakura/Legends|Bakura]] a few [[Standard day/Legends|days]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].<ref name="TAB">''[[The Truce at Bakura]]''</ref> The Ssi-ruuk's [[Human/Legends|Human]] captive, [[Dev Sibwarra]], carried an ion paddle beamer that had been specially modified for his smaller grip.<ref name="TABSB" />

After the successful repulsion of the Ssi-ruuk invasion force, [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance]] forces captured some of these weapons, but they proved incompatible with standard technology.<ref name="EGWT" /> Connecting a stand [[Blaster power pack/Legends|blaster power pack]] to a paddle beamer immediately burnt out the weapon. Luke Skywalker destroyed a majority of the samples to release the enteched spirits that powered them, but a few remained in the hands of Alliance [[Technician/Legends|technicians]] for study. These technicians believed that it was impossible to duplicate the weapons based on their limited knowledge of Ssi-ruuk technology.<ref name="TABSB" />

Almost twenty-five years later, during the height of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], the Ssi-ruuk again tried to [[Invasion of Bakura|invade]] Bakura, however their technology had not seen radical improvement and the paddle beamer still operated on the same principles.<ref name="FH2">''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic II: Refugee]]''</ref>
